compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: DECISION CONFIRMED (user, 2026-07-11): failure posture is FAIL-CLOSED — a detected refusal-as-content raises a typed UnretryableOutputError so the LLM gate BLOCKS (never reads a refusal as a PASS). Retry is acceptable only as an add-on (retry then fail closed); the terminal state is always closed, not open. This extends the existing check_stop_reason typed-error path to the content level for consistency.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: CHARACTERIZATION COMPLETE (2026-07-11): rebar's structured-output path is NOT vulnerable to refusal-as-content on the current stack. Determination: pydantic-ai 1.107.0's Anthropic model (models/anthropic.py) maps Anthropic stop_reason='refusal' -> finish_reason='content_filter' via _FINISH_REASON_MAP (line 95) and sets it on the ModelResponse (lines 951-953), also stashing the refusal explanation in provider_details['refusal'] (line 957). rebar's structured.check_stop_reason treats BOTH 'refusal' and 'content_filter' as _UNRETRYABLE_STOP_REASONS, and the runner calls it on BOTH output paths (runner.py NativeOutput + PromptedOutput) BEFORE returning/parsing output. So a refusal already raises UnretryableOutputError -> the gate degrades to INDETERMINATE, never a hollow PASS. The #5221 gap (refusal as content with a benign finish_reason) does NOT reproduce here because pydantic-ai populates finish_reason from stop_reason. Cites pydantic-ai #5221 / #6167. RESOLUTION = characterized-not-vulnerable, with two hardening additions per the fail-closed decision: (1) a NEW structured.check_response(response) that layers a defense-in-depth read of provider_details (raw 'refusal'/'finish_reason') on top of check_stop_reason, so rebar stays fail-closed EVEN IF a future adapter stops mapping a refusal onto finish_reason (the exact #5221 shape); the runner now calls check_response on both paths. (2) A regression/contract test (tests/unit/test_refusal_as_content.py) that imports pydantic-ai's _FINISH_REASON_MAP and asserts Anthropic 'refusal' still maps into rebar's catch set — so a future pydantic-ai remap that would silently reopen the gap FAILS the test loudly. Verified: 7 new tests + silent-success/structured/llm-failure suites (139 tests) pass; ruff + mypy clean.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

Characterize — and, only if a gap is confirmed, detect — the **refusal-as-content** failure
mode in rebar's LLM structured-output path: a provider content-policy refusal returned as a
normal-looking TEXT/verdict rather than raising, so an LLM gate (review / verify-completion /
plan-review) could read a refusal as a valid result.

## Why (background)

pydantic-ai **#5221** (+ fix PR **#6167**) reports that a content-policy refusal can come back
as a plain text part with `response.status='incomplete'` / `incomplete_details.reason=
'content_filter'` and NO structured refusal object. pydantic-ai's own agent loop only raises
`ContentFilterError` when the parts are **empty**, so a refusal-as-content (non-empty
`TextPart`) is returned to the caller as normal output.

**Note on provider:** #5221 is about **OpenAI** (`finish_reason='content_filter'`), not
Anthropic — this ticket's title conflates them. rebar runs Anthropic (`claude-*`), where the
analogous signal is `stop_reason="refusal"` (see `src/rebar/llm/errors.py:40`,
`src/rebar/llm/structured.py:231`). The failure *shape* (a refusal surfaced as content) is
provider-independent; the characterization must confirm which signals Anthropic-via-pydantic-ai
actually emits for a refusal.

**rebar already has a partial defense** (from closed story 978d,
`jade`-adjacent silent-success work): `structured.check_stop_reason(stop_reason)`
(`src/rebar/llm/structured.py:249`) raises `UnretryableOutputError` on
`{refusal, max_tokens, length, content_filter}`, and the runner calls it on BOTH output paths
(`runner.py:519` PromptedOutput, `runner.py:539` NativeOutput) reading
`run_result.response.finish_reason`. So IF pydantic-ai reliably surfaces
`finish_reason`/`stop_reason` for a refusal-as-content turn, rebar already fails closed. The
open question this ticket answers is whether that signal is reliably present, or whether a
refusal can slip through with an unset/`stop` finish_reason.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Construct (or capture via a cassette) a pydantic-ai `ModelResponse` representing an
   Anthropic refusal-as-content turn: a non-empty `TextPart` carrying refusal prose. Vary the
   `finish_reason`/`stop_reason` across `{"refusal", None, "stop", "content_filter"}`.
2. Drive it through rebar's structured path (`runner.py` NativeOutput + PromptedOutput →
   `structured.check_stop_reason`) for a gated op (e.g. `verify_completion`).
3. Observe whether rebar RAISES (`UnretryableOutputError`) or returns the refusal prose as a
   (hollow but valid-looking) verdict.

- **Expected:** any refusal-as-content turn fails closed — rebar raises a typed refusal error
  and never returns the refusal as a usable verdict/review result.
- **Actual (to be characterized):** unknown. If `finish_reason` is reliably `refusal`/
  `content_filter`, `check_stop_reason` already catches it (no gap). If a refusal can arrive
  with `finish_reason` unset/`stop`, the refusal prose is returned as content — the #5221 gap
  reproduced in rebar.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Characterized:** a documented determination (a comment/artifact on this ticket, plus a
  reproduction test) of whether rebar's structured path is vulnerable to refusal-as-content —
  i.e., whether a refusal turn can be returned as a non-empty valid-looking result that
  `check_stop_reason` does NOT catch. Cites pydantic-ai #5221 / #6167 and the exact rebar code
  path (`structured.check_stop_reason`, `runner.py:519/539`).
- [ ] **If a gap is confirmed:** add a content-level guard (in `src/rebar/llm/structured.py`
  and/or `runner.py`) that recognizes a refusal-as-content response **independent of
  finish_reason** and raises the existing `UnretryableOutputError` (fail-closed), so a refusal
  can never be returned as a valid verdict/review. Covered by a unit test that simulates a
  refusal-as-content `ModelResponse` with an unset finish_reason and asserts it raises.
- [ ] **If NO gap** (the finish_reason/stop_reason signal is reliably present for our provider
  and `check_stop_reason` already fails closed): document why, add a **regression test** that
  pins that a refusal-as-content turn raises (so a future pydantic-ai/provider change can't
  silently reopen the gap), and close as characterized-not-vulnerable.
- [ ] File paths: `src/rebar/llm/structured.py`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`,
  `src/rebar/llm/errors.py`; tests under `tests/unit/`.

## Notes / decisions to confirm on review

- Desired posture is **fail-closed** (a refusal → typed error, never a PASS) per the user's
  earlier direction. Confirm this over warn-only or retry.
- Scope is the structured/verdict path (the LLM gates). Non-structured/free-text LLM calls (if
  any) are out of scope unless the characterization shows they share the defect.
compiled_state.title: Characterize + detect refusal-as-content in the structured-output path (pydantic-ai #5221)
